channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Word of the Day: solace
item.description: This word has appeared in 417 articles on NYTimes.com in the past year. Can you use it in a sentence?
item.dc:creator: The Learning Network

item.title: What the Extraordinary Market Volatility in Asia Says About Energy and A.I.
item.description: Stocks in South Korea and Taiwan, the center of global chip making, plunged on fears about energy prices. Their recovery shows the bullishness over artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Meaghan Tobin
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ahn Young-Joon/Associated Press
media:content.media:description: Currency traders at the Hana Bank headquarters in Seoul on Thursday.

item.title: On the Ground in Beirut
item.description: Our Beirut bureau chief, Christina Goldbaum, reports on the escalating conflict between Israel and the Iran-backed armed group Hezbollah, as Israel’s military seizes areas of southern Lebanon and carries out bombings.
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Senate Republicans Block Limits to Trump’s War Powers
item.description: Senate Republicans voted against a Democratic bill that would have required President Trump to obtain congressional authorization to continue waging war against Iran.
item.dc:creator: Shawn Paik
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The rubble of a police station in Tehran this week after the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: Australia’s Sydney Jewish Museum to Memorialize Bondi Beach Massacre
item.description: The country and its small Jewish community are still trying to process the mass shooting at Bondi Beach in December.
item.dc:creator: Laura Chung and Matthew Abbott

media:content.media:credit: Matthew Abbott for The New York Times

item.title: Corrections: March 5, 2026
item.description: Corrections that appeared in print on Thursday, March 5, 2026.

item.title: Quote of the Day: Global Leaders Brace For Fallout From War
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Thursday, March 5, 2026.

item.title: In a Riskier Era, China Bets on Technology to Resist U.S. Pressure
item.description: China announced a 7 percent increase in military spending and a five-year plan to try to reduce its military and industry’s reliance on Western technology.
item.dc:creator: Chris Buckley and Lily Kuo
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Lintao Zhang/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Xi Jinping, China’s leader, at the annual meeting of the national legislature in Beijing on Thursday.

item.title: James Talarico Says ‘New Politics Is Being Born’ After Texas Primary Win
item.description: After clinching the Democratic Senate primary, he told supporters in Austin that the country’s real divide is between “top versus bottom,” not the left versus right.
item.dc:creator: Ramón Ramirez

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Talarico speaking to supporters in Austin, Texas, on Wednesday night.

item.title: Several of Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer’s employees are under investigation for official misconduct under her leadership.
item.description: Several of Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer’s employees are under investigation for official misconduct under her leadership.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Davis O’Brien

media:content.media:credit: Al Drago for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: An aide to Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer was placed on administrative leave Wednesday.

item.title: Sarah J. Maas Announces Next Books in ‘A Court of Thorns and Roses’ Series
item.description: The sixth book is scheduled to be released on Oct. 27, 2026, and the seventh on Jan. 12, 2027, the author announced on the “Call Her Daddy” podcast.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler
item.category: A Court of Thorns and Roses (Book)

media:content.media:credit: Cindy Ord/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Sarah J. Maas did not share the titles or cover art for her new books, but acknowledged that the new installments had been “a long time coming.”

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for March 5, 2026
item.description: Hanh Huynh picks a winner.
item.dc:creator: Rian Johnson

media:content.media:credit: Jacques Demarthon/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: It’s always nice to spend a Sunday — or a Thursday — in the park with a puzzle.

item.title: Contract for El Paso ICE Center Is Under Review, Homeland Security Says
item.description: Camp East Montana in Texas has been under growing scrutiny over its living conditions and has been grappling with a measles outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Madeleine Ngo

media:content.media:credit: Paul Ratje for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Camp East Montana, a U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement detention facility at Fort Bliss in El Paso.

item.title: Por qué Ecuador pidió ayuda al ejército de EE. UU. en su lucha contra el narcotráfico
item.description: El presidente Daniel Noboa es uno de los más firmes partidarios de Estados Unidos en la región y parece más dispuesto a acoger a sus fuerzas militares que otros dirigentes de América Latina.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, Eric Schmitt and José María León Cabrera
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Daniele Volpe para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El ejército estadounidense está ayudando a Ecuador a planificar una serie de redadas contra las violentas pandillas de narcotraficantes del país sudamericano.

item.title: Man Accused of Plotting to Kill Trump Blames Iran for Scheme
item.description: Asif Merchant testified in his own defense, saying he participated in the plot to protect his family in Iran. Prosecutors reject his account of his motives.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A plot to kill President Trump or former President Joseph R. Biden Jr. had little chance of success, a man charged with terrorism and murder for hire testified.

item.title: Daines Drops Re-election Bid in Montana, Upending a Senate Race
item.description: The surprise announcement by Senator Steve Daines could give Democrats a slim opening in the Republican-leaning state in their uphill fight to take the Senate majority.
item.dc:creator: Kellen Browning

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Senator Steve Daines said it was time for new leaders from Montana and endorsed a Republican successor.

item.title: ۴۰ پزشک و پرستار ایرانی از یک قتل‌عام می‌گویند.
item.description: ما از طریق یک پرسشنامه، تجربهٔ کادر درمان ایران در ۱۴ شهر و ۱۱ استان، از درمان معترضان زخمی را ثبت کردیم.
item.dc:creator: Roxana Saberi and Fatemeh Jamalpour

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Man Killed After Fleeing Texas Border Patrol Checkpoint, Police Say
item.description: The man fired shots at law enforcement and civilian vehicles before he was killed during a pursuit along Interstate 10 in West Texas.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler

media:content.media:credit: Herika Martinez/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Border Patrol checkpoint in Sierra Blanca, Texas, last year.

item.title: Progressive Challenger Concedes in N.C.’s Bluest Congressional District
item.description: The Associated Press had not yet called the race. The contest was widely viewed as a bellwether for the type of candidate that Democratic voters wished to elevate in the Trump era.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina

media:content.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Representative Valerie Foushee, who was first elected in 2022, faced a challenge from her left in the Democratic primary for North Carolina’s Fourth Congressional District.

item.title: China Sets Economy’s Growth Target Below 5% for First Time in Decades
item.description: The goal, announced at a gathering of Communist Party leaders, was the lowest since 1991 and can offer clues about China’s policymaking plans.
item.dc:creator: Aaron Krolik

media:content.media:credit: Florence Lo/Reuters
media:content.media:description: China’s leader, Xi Jinping, at the Great Hall of the People in Beijing on Wednesday.

item.title: Trump Follows His Gut. His National Security Advisers Try to Keep Up.
item.description: Decisions come fast, even if contradictions and inconsistencies abound. But without much of a process, there is little preparation for how things can go wrong.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Donald Trump in the Oval Office on Tuesday. His decision to attack Iran, he said, was based on gut instinct.

item.title: U.S. Court Takes First Steps Toward Ordering Tariff Refunds
item.description: The Trump administration is likely to appeal the move, as it ramps up its attempt to slow or potentially block the repayment of billions in past duties.
item.dc:creator: Tony Romm and Ana Swanson

media:content.media:credit: Ruth Fremson/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: For many importers, the liquidation process is still well underway for millions of goods that were brought ashore during President Trump’s first year back in office.

item.title: Pro-American Kurdish Forces Are Preparing Possible Iran Incursion
item.description: The C.I.A. has given small weapons to Iranian Kurdish forces in Iraq in a covert program that began before the current war.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon, Julian E. Barnes, Christiaan Triebert, Parin Behrooz and Farnaz Fassihi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Safin Hamid/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: A member of the Kurdistan Democratic Party of Iran inspects the damage sustained at the party’s Azadi Camp after an Iranian cross-border attack in the Kurdish region of northern Iraq.

item.title: Judge Blocks DeSantis’s Declaration of Muslim Group as Terrorist Organization
item.description: A federal judge found that the designation for CAIR, one of the nation’s largest Muslim advocacy groups, had violated the organization’s First Amendment rights.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei

media:content.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Gov. Ron DeSantis of Florida signed an executive order in December declaring the Council on American-Islamic Relations a terrorist group.

item.title: Druski Wants to Make a Buddy Cop Movie With Timothée Chalamet
item.description: The comedian Druski sat down with Jon Caramanica and Joe Coscarelli on “Popcast” for a revealing conversation about his galaxy of collaborators and the balancing act between unfiltered comedy and corporate America. A buddy cop movie with Timothée Chalamet? Don’t rule it out.
item.dc:creator: Popcast

item.title: Whose Airstrike Hit a Girls’ School in Iran? The U.S. Says It’s Still Investigating.
item.description: The strike was one of the deadliest attacks of the American-Israeli campaign against Iran so far.
item.dc:creator: Shawn McCreesh
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
United Nations Educational, Scientific and Cultural Organization

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Karoline Leavitt, the White House press secretary, was asked on Wednesday if the United States had conducted the airstrike on an Iranian school.

item.title: Why Ecuador Invited the U.S. Military to Help With Its Drug Gangs
item.description: Drug gangs have turned the South American country into one of the most dangerous in the region and the world’s leading exporter of cocaine.
item.dc:creator: Luis Ferré-Sadurní, Eric Schmitt and José María León Cabrera
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:credit: Daniele Volpe for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The Ecuadorian Coast Guard patrolling in 2025. The U.S. military is now helping Ecuador plan a series of raids targeting the South American country’s violent drug gangs.

item.title: Noem and D.H.S. Inspector General Spar Over Obstruction Claim
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem pushed back against her department’s inspector general, who told Congress in a letter that she had “systematically obstructed” his office’s work.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian, Hamed Aleaziz and Michael Gold
item.category: Homeland Security Office of Inspector General

media:content.media:credit: Kevin Dietsch/Getty Images
media:content.media:description: The Homeland Security Department’s inspector general, Joseph V. Cuffari, testifying last year. Mr. Cuffari has opened investigations that include an audit of how Immigration and Customs Enforcement investigates use-of-force incidents.

item.title: What to Know About the Texas Runoffs in May: Key Races and Candidates
item.description: Texas voters will revisit the Republican Senate primary — and some House races where no candidate captured more than 50 percent of the vote — in runoffs on May 26.
item.dc:creator: Tim Balk

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Voters in line to cast their ballots at a polling site in Austin, Texas, on Tuesday.

item.title: Israelis Celebrate Purim Amid Iranian Missile Attacks
item.description: The war did not stop ultra-Orthodox parts of Jerusalem from drinking and dancing for the Jewish holiday.
item.dc:creator: Natan Odenheimer

media:content.media:credit: Avishag Shaar-Yashuv for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Celebrating Purim inside a bomb shelter in Tel Aviv on Monday.

item.title: What’s Next for Texas Under Trump? 4 Big Questions.
item.description: The biggest immediate one: What will Trump do?
item.dc:creator: Katie Glueck

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: James Talarico’s election night party in Austin, Texas.

item.title: Yakuza Leader Sentenced to 20 Years for Trafficking Nuclear Material
item.description: Prosecutors said Takeshi Ebisawa tried to sell uranium and plutonium from Myanmar in a plot tied to Iran’s nuclear program.
item.dc:creator: Mark Walker

media:content.media:credit: United States Justice Department
media:content.media:description: Takeshi Ebisawa holding a rocket launcher during a meeting with undercover officers in Copenhagen in 2021.

item.title: Former Barclays Center Executive Says Live Nation Threatened to Pull Tours
item.description: The Justice Department had John Abbamondi testify at an antitrust trial to support its case that Live Nation has acted as a monopoly, an accusation the company denies.
item.dc:creator: Ben Sisario

media:content.media:credit: Vincent Alban/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Barclays Center in Brooklyn was the subject of heated testimony at the Live Nation trial on Wednesday.

item.title: Elon Musk Defends Social Media Posts in Twitter Shareholder Lawsuit
item.description: Mr. Musk said he did not expect his posts in the lead-up to his 2022 acquisition of the social media site, now X, to affect the company’s share price.
item.dc:creator: Kate Conger

media:content.media:credit: Jason Henry for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Elon Musk arriving at the Phillip Burton Federal Building and U.S. Courthouse in San Francisco on Wednesday.

item.title: The U.S. Is Accelerating Its Attacks on Iran
item.description: Also, sea levels may be higher than we thought. Here’s the latest at the end of Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Smoke rises over Tehran today after U.S.-Israel airstrikes.

item.title: As Attacks Spread, War With Iran Reaches Far Beyond Mideast
item.description: NATO downed a missile fired by Iran at Turkey, and a U.S. sub sank an Iranian Navy ship off Sri Lanka, thousands of miles from the war zone.
item.dc:creator: Eric Schmitt, Helene Cooper, Lara Jakes and Michael Levenson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Damage in Tehran has been extensive, including at the police station in the background on Wednesday.

item.title: 59% of Americans Oppose the Military Action in Iran
item.description: Three high-quality polls taken after the United States and Israel launched their attack Iran on Saturday show that a majority of Americans are not on board with the military action.
item.dc:creator: Ruth Igielnik

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: A police station in Iran after U.S. and Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: Why Democrats Are Encouraged by the U.S. Senate Primaries in Texas
item.description: Our national political correspondent Shane Goldmacher describes how James Talarico won the Democratic nomination for Senate in Texas. The Republican incumbent, Senator John Cornyn, was forced into a runoff with the state’s attorney general, Ken Paxton.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher, Gabriel Blanco and James Surdam

item.title: Los demócratas eligen a Talarico en Texas y los republicanos enfilan a una segunda vuelta
item.description: James Talarico ganó las primarias demócratas del estado para el Senado, mientras que el senador John Cornyn se enfrenta a una segunda vuelta contra Ken Paxton, su contrincante de derecha.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: People waiting for Mr. Talarico to speak at his watch party in Austin, Texas.

item.title: Talarico Can Win. But He Will Not Turn Texas Blue.
item.description: Texas politics is in constant churn.
item.dc:creator: Christopher Hooks and John Guida

media:content.media:credit: Brenda Bazán/Associated Press

item.title: Trump Announces A.I. Industry Pledge to Pay for Power
item.description: Companies including Google, Microsoft and OpenAI committed to pay for the power plants and grid upgrades needed to run their data centers.
item.dc:creator: David McCabe and Brad Plumer

media:content.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
media:content.media:description: President Trump meeting with executives from the artificial intelligence industry in Washington on Wednesday.

item.title: Senate Thwarts Bid to Curb Trump’s War Powers on Iran
item.description: Nearly every Republican voted to block a measure that would require that President Trump win authorization from Congress to continue the offensive in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Robert Jimison
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The rubble of a police station in Tehran this week after the U.S.-Israeli airstrikes.

item.title: James Talarico Wins the Texas Democratic Senate Race. The Real Challenge Lies Ahead.
item.description: After the state representative won the Senate primary, he joined a list of other promising progressives who have captured the left’s fancy.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.category: Late Show with Stephen Colbert (TV Program)

media:content.media:credit: Tamir Kalifa for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: State Representative James Talarico has emerged as the Texas Democratic nominee for the Senate and is suddenly one of the hottest properties in the party.

item.title: Iranian TV and Social Media Project Defiant and Distorted View of the War
item.description: State media and online propagandists are striking a confident posture, despite heavy losses. Some of the content was generated by artificial intelligence.
item.dc:creator: Tiffany Hsu, Steven Lee Myers and Stuart A. Thompson
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Senate Vote Count: Iran War Powers Resolution
item.description: The Senate rejected on a measure to rein in the use of military force in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Martín González Gómez, Karen Yourish and Alicia Parlapiano

media:content.media:credit: The New York Times

item.title: Newsom Compares Israel to ‘Apartheid State’
item.description: The California governor, seen as a likely presidential candidate, made comments that reflect a shift in the Democratic Party.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Medina

media:content.media:credit: Jessica Christian/San Francisco Chronicle, via Associated Press
media:content.media:description: California Gov. Gavin Newsom at a news conference on Monday. Mr. Newsom has for years been a firm supporter of Israel, but he has shifted his stance recently.

item.title: Travelers Stranded by U.S.-Israel Conflict With Iran Face Endless Flight Cancellations
item.description: Even far from hubs like Dubai and Abu Dhabi, the disruptions from the growing violence have left people jumping “from one cancellation to the next.”
item.dc:creator: Christine Chung, Gabe Castro-Root and Niraj Chokshi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Ryan Lim/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Tourists in Abu Dhabi, in the United Arab Emirates. on Sunday, the day after the United States and Israel struck Iran, setting off a conflict that has caused chaos for travelers.

item.title: Women and Leadership: 9 Figures Discuss Their Approach
item.description: A chef, a disability advocate, a tech executive, a ballerina. They and five other women share what drives and challenges them as leaders.
item.dc:creator: Shivani Vora

item.title: Feminist Scholars and Activists on the Past and Future of Feminism
item.description: Feminist scholars and activists, including Gloria Steinem and Leymah Gbowee, reflected on a movement that they said remains unfinished.
item.dc:creator: Tanya Mohn

item.title: Live Updates: Senate Republicans Block War Powers Limits as Mideast Crisis Widens
item.description: The Defense Department released the name of a fifth American killed in an Iranian attack on Sunday, and released the name of another soldier believed to have died in the same incident. The State Department said a charter flight was returning to the U.S. with Americans wanting to leave the region.
item.dc:creator: The New York Times

media:content.media:credit: Hassan Ammar/Associated Press
